title: Viola micrantha lusus austroussuriensis
taxonomy: Viola micrantha lusus austroussuriensis is a subspecies of the Viola micrantha species, which belongs to the family Violaceae. It is classified within the genus Viola, which contains a large number of flowering plant species. The classification of Viola micrantha lusus austroussuriensis is based on its unique characteristics and geographical distribution. This subspecies is likely to be further classified into a specific taxonomic rank within the Violaceae family. The classification of Viola micrantha lusus austroussuriensis provides valuable information about its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.

intro: Viola micrantha lusus austroussuriensis, also known as the small pansy of the Eastern Ussuri Mountains, is a subspecies of the Viola micrantha plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the violet family (Violaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, protect against oxidative stress, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Viola micrantha lusus austroussuriensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
